steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says the album received critical attention and praise, then states "few were surprised" when it won a Grammy. The blank must connect a positive reception to an unsurprising award win — a cause-and-effect relationship.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Often,: signals frequency, not a consequence. It doesn't explain why the win was expected. ✗
  • Choice B — Therefore,: signals that what follows is a direct result of what came before. The critical praise logically caused the win to be unsurprising. ✓
  • Choice C — Nevertheless,: signals contrast or surprise, the opposite of what the passage sets up. ✗
  • Choice D — Granted,: signals a concession, implying the writer is acknowledging a counterpoint. No counterpoint exists here. ✗
That matches Choice B — Therefore,.
